    def __init__(self, api_client=None) -> None:
    if api_client is None:
        api_client = ApiClient.get_default()
    self.api_client = api_client

    order_approve_post #

    order_approve_post(
    order_id: Annotated[
        Optional[StrictStr],
        Field(description="The id of the order to approve"),
    ] = None,
    _request_timeout: Union[
        None,
        Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
        Tuple[
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],

    _request_auth: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _content_type: Optional[StrictStr] = None,
    _headers: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _host_index: Annotated[
        StrictInt, Field(ge=0, le=0)
    ] = 0,
) -> None

    Approves an order that has been submitted for manual approval.

    An order is submitted for manual approval when the automatic approval process could not automatically determine if the order is safe to process. Once an order is approved, the pipeline will start processing the order.

    :param order_id: The id of the order to approve :type order_id: str :param _request_timeout: timeout setting for this request. If one number provided, it will be total request timeout. It can also be a pair (tuple) of (connection, read) timeouts. :type _request_timeout: int, tuple(int, int), optional :param _request_auth: set to override the auth_settings for an a single request; this effectively ignores the authentication in the spec for a single request. :type _request_auth: dict, optional :param _content_type: force content-type for the request. :type _content_type: str, Optional :param _headers: set to override the headers for a single request; this effectively ignores the headers in the spec for a single request. :type _headers: dict, optional :param _host_index: set to override the host_index for a single request; this effectively ignores the host_index in the spec for a single request. :type _host_index: int, optional :return: Returns the result object.

def order_approve_post(
    self,
    order_id: Annotated[Optional[StrictStr], Field(description="The id of the order to approve")] = None,
    _request_timeout: Union[
        None,
        Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
        Tuple[
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)]
        ]
    ] = None,
    _request_auth: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _content_type: Optional[StrictStr] = None,
    _headers: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _host_index: Annotated[StrictInt, Field(ge=0, le=0)] = 0,
) -> None:
    """Approves an order that has been submitted for manual approval.

    An order is submitted for manual approval when the automatic approval process could not automatically  determine if the order is safe to process.  Once an order is approved, the pipeline will start processing the order.

    :param order_id: The id of the order to approve
    :type order_id: str
    :param _request_timeout: timeout setting for this request. If one
                             number provided, it will be total request
                             timeout. It can also be a pair (tuple) of
                             (connection, read) timeouts.
    :type _request_timeout: int, tuple(int, int), optional
    :param _request_auth: set to override the auth_settings for an a single
                          request; this effectively ignores the
                          authentication in the spec for a single request.
    :type _request_auth: dict, optional
    :param _content_type: force content-type for the request.
    :type _content_type: str, Optional
    :param _headers: set to override the headers for a single
                     request; this effectively ignores the headers
                     in the spec for a single request.
    :type _headers: dict, optional
    :param _host_index: set to override the host_index for a single
                        request; this effectively ignores the host_index
                        in the spec for a single request.
    :type _host_index: int, optional
    :return: Returns the result object.
    """ # noqa: E501

    _param = self._order_approve_post_serialize(
        order_id=order_id,
        _request_auth=_request_auth,
        _content_type=_content_type,
        _headers=_headers,
        _host_index=_host_index
    )

    _response_types_map: Dict[str, Optional[str]] = {
        '200': None,
    }
    response_data = self.api_client.call_api(
        *_param,
        _request_timeout=_request_timeout
    )
    response_data.read()
    return self.api_client.response_deserialize(
        response_data=response_data,
        response_types_map=_response_types_map,
    ).data

def order_approve_post_with_http_info(
    self,
    order_id: Annotated[Optional[StrictStr], Field(description="The id of the order to approve")] = None,
    _request_timeout: Union[
        None,
        Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
        Tuple[

    _request_auth: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,

    _headers: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _host_index: Annotated[StrictInt, Field(ge=0, le=0)] = 0,

    """Approves an order that has been submitted for manual approval.

    An order is submitted for manual approval when the automatic approval process could not automatically  determine if the order is safe to process.  Once an order is approved, the pipeline will start processing the order.

    :param order_id: The id of the order to approve
    :type order_id: str
    :param _request_timeout: timeout setting for this request. If one
                             number provided, it will be total request

    :type _request_timeout: int, tuple(int, int), optional

    :type _content_type: str, Optional

                     request; this effectively ignores the headers
                     in the spec for a single request.
    :type _headers: dict, optional
    :param _host_index: set to override the host_index for a single

                        in the spec for a single request.
    :type _host_index: int, optional
    :return: Returns the result object.

    response_data.read()

    clone_order_model: Annotated[

            description="The body content of the request"

    Clones an existing order.

    In order to clone an order, the existing order must be marked as public.

    :param clone_order_model: The body content of the request :type clone_order_model: CloneOrderModel :param _request_timeout: timeout setting for this request. If one number provided, it will be total request timeout. It can also be a pair (tuple) of (connection, read) timeouts. :type _request_timeout: int, tuple(int, int), optional :param _request_auth: set to override the auth_settings for an a single request; this effectively ignores the authentication in the spec for a single request. :type _request_auth: dict, optional :param _content_type: force content-type for the request. :type _content_type: str, Optional :param _headers: set to override the headers for a single request; this effectively ignores the headers in the spec for a single request. :type _headers: dict, optional :param _host_index: set to override the host_index for a single request; this effectively ignores the host_index in the spec for a single request. :type _host_index: int, optional :return: Returns the result object.

    @validate_call
def order_clone_order_post(
    self,
    clone_order_model: Annotated[Optional[CloneOrderModel], Field(description="The body content of the request")] = None,
    _request_timeout: Union[
        None,
        Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
        Tuple[
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)]
        ]
    ] = None,
    _request_auth: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _content_type: Optional[StrictStr] = None,
    _headers: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _host_index: Annotated[StrictInt, Field(ge=0, le=0)] = 0,
) -> CloneOrderResult:
    """Clones an existing order.

    In order to clone an order, the existing order must be marked as public.

    :param clone_order_model: The body content of the request
    :type clone_order_model: CloneOrderModel
    :param _request_timeout: timeout setting for this request. If one
                             number provided, it will be total request
                             timeout. It can also be a pair (tuple) of
                             (connection, read) timeouts.
    :type _request_timeout: int, tuple(int, int), optional
    :param _request_auth: set to override the auth_settings for an a single
                          request; this effectively ignores the
                          authentication in the spec for a single request.
    :type _request_auth: dict, optional
    :param _content_type: force content-type for the request.
    :type _content_type: str, Optional
    :param _headers: set to override the headers for a single
                     request; this effectively ignores the headers
                     in the spec for a single request.
    :type _headers: dict, optional
    :param _host_index: set to override the host_index for a single
                        request; this effectively ignores the host_index
                        in the spec for a single request.
    :type _host_index: int, optional
    :return: Returns the result object.
    """ # noqa: E501

    _param = self._order_clone_order_post_serialize(
        clone_order_model=clone_order_model,
        _request_auth=_request_auth,
        _content_type=_content_type,
        _headers=_headers,
        _host_index=_host_index
    )

    _response_types_map: Dict[str, Optional[str]] = {
        '200': "CloneOrderResult",
    }
    response_data = self.api_client.call_api(
        *_param,
        _request_timeout=_request_timeout
    )
    response_data.read()
    return self.api_client.response_deserialize(
        response_data=response_data,
        response_types_map=_response_types_map,
    ).data

    Resumes a paused order. Meaning all campaigns in the order will be resumed.

    :param order_id: :type order_id: str :param _request_timeout: timeout setting for this request. If one number provided, it will be total request timeout.     Submits an order for approval.

    Once an order is created its order id and dataset id is returned. The dataset id can be used to fill the dataset with data points. Once submitted, the order will be locked and no further changes can be made.

    :param order_id: The id of the order to submit :type order_id: str :param _request_timeout: timeout setting for this request. If one number provided, it will be total request timeout.     @validate_call
def order_submit_post(
    self,
    order_id: Annotated[Optional[StrictStr], Field(description="The id of the order to submit")] = None,
    _request_timeout: Union[
        None,
        Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
        Tuple[
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)]
        ]
    ] = None,
    _request_auth: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _content_type: Optional[StrictStr] = None,
    _headers: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _host_index: Annotated[StrictInt, Field(ge=0, le=0)] = 0,
) -> None:
    """Submits an order for approval.

    Once an order is created its order id and dataset id is returned.  The dataset id can be used to fill the dataset with data points.  Once submitted, the order will be locked and no further changes can be made.

    :param order_id: The id of the order to submit
    :type order_id: str
    :param _request_timeout: timeout setting for this request. If one
                             number provided, it will be total request
                             timeout. It can also be a pair (tuple) of
                             (connection, read) timeouts.
    :type _request_timeout: int, tuple(int, int), optional
    :param _request_auth: set to override the auth_settings for an a single
                          request; this effectively ignores the
                          authentication in the spec for a single request.
    :type _request_auth: dict, optional
    :param _content_type: force content-type for the request.
    :type _content_type: str, Optional
    :param _headers: set to override the headers for a single
                     request; this effectively ignores the headers
                     in the spec for a single request.
    :type _headers: dict, optional
    :param _host_index: set to override the host_index for a single
                        request; this effectively ignores the host_index
                        in the spec for a single request.
    :type _host_index: int, optional
    :return: Returns the result object.
    """ # noqa: E501

    _param = self._order_submit_post_serialize(
        order_id=order_id,
        _request_auth=_request_auth,
        _content_type=_content_type,
        _headers=_headers,
        _host_index=_host_index
    )

    _response_types_map: Dict[str, Optional[str]] = {
        '200': None,
    }
    response_data = self.api_client.call_api(
        *_param,
        _request_timeout=_request_timeout
    )
    response_data.read()
    return self.api_client.response_deserialize(
        response_data=response_data,
        response_types_map=_response_types_map,
    ).data

    order_unlock_post #

    order_unlock_post(
    order_id: Annotated[
        Optional[StrictStr],
        Field(description="The id of the order to unlock"),
    ] = None,
    _request_timeout: Union[
        None,
        Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
        Tuple[
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
        ],
    ] = None,
    _request_auth: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _content_type: Optional[StrictStr] = None,
    _headers: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _host_index: Annotated[
        StrictInt, Field(ge=0, le=0)
    ] = 0,
) -> UnlockOrderResult

    Unlocks a newly cloned order.

    When an order is cloned only the necessary information is copied. In order to make changes to the order, it must be unlocked first. Unlocking the order will clone the entire dataset and its datapoints.

    :param order_id: The id of the order to unlock :type order_id: str :param _request_timeout: timeout setting for this request. If one number provided, it will be total request timeout. It can also be a pair (tuple) of (connection, read) timeouts. :type _request_timeout: int, tuple(int, int), optional :param _request_auth: set to override the auth_settings for an a single request; this effectively ignores the authentication in the spec for a single request. :type _request_auth: dict, optional :param _content_type: force content-type for the request. :type _content_type: str, Optional :param _headers: set to override the headers for a single request; this effectively ignores the headers in the spec for a single request. :type _headers: dict, optional :param _host_index: set to override the host_index for a single request; this effectively ignores the host_index in the spec for a single request. :type _host_index: int, optional :return: Returns the result object.

    @validate_call
def order_unlock_post(
    self,
    order_id: Annotated[Optional[StrictStr], Field(description="The id of the order to unlock")] = None,
    _request_timeout: Union[
        None,
        Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
        Tuple[
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)]
        ]
    ] = None,
    _request_auth: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _content_type: Optional[StrictStr] = None,
    _headers: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _host_index: Annotated[StrictInt, Field(ge=0, le=0)] = 0,
) -> UnlockOrderResult:
    """Unlocks a newly cloned order.

    When an order is cloned only the necessary information is copied.  In order to make changes to the order, it must be unlocked first.  Unlocking the order will clone the entire dataset and its datapoints.

    :param order_id: The id of the order to unlock
    :type order_id: str
    :param _request_timeout: timeout setting for this request. If one
                             number provided, it will be total request
                             timeout. It can also be a pair (tuple) of
                             (connection, read) timeouts.
    :type _request_timeout: int, tuple(int, int), optional
    :param _request_auth: set to override the auth_settings for an a single
                          request; this effectively ignores the
                          authentication in the spec for a single request.
    :type _request_auth: dict, optional
    :param _content_type: force content-type for the request.
    :type _content_type: str, Optional
    :param _headers: set to override the headers for a single
                     request; this effectively ignores the headers
                     in the spec for a single request.
    :type _headers: dict, optional
    :param _host_index: set to override the host_index for a single
                        request; this effectively ignores the host_index
                        in the spec for a single request.
    :type _host_index: int, optional
    :return: Returns the result object.
    """ # noqa: E501

    _param = self._order_unlock_post_serialize(
        order_id=order_id,
        _request_auth=_request_auth,
        _content_type=_content_type,
        _headers=_headers,
        _host_index=_host_index
    )

    _response_types_map: Dict[str, Optional[str]] = {
        '200': "UnlockOrderResult",
    }
    response_data = self.api_client.call_api(
        *_param,
        _request_timeout=_request_timeout
    )
    response_data.read()
    return self.api_client.response_deserialize(
        response_data=response_data,
        response_types_map=_response_types_map,
    ).data

    Unlocks a newly cloned order.

    When an order is cloned only the necessary information is copied. In order to make changes to the order, it must be unlocked first. Unlocking the order will clone the entire dataset and its datapoints.

    :param order_id: The id of the order to unlock :type order_id: str :param _request_timeout: timeout setting for this request. If one number provided, it will be total request timeout. def order_unlock_post_with_http_info(
    self,
    order_id: Annotated[Optional[StrictStr], Field(description="The id of the order to unlock")] = None,
    _request_timeout: Union[
        None,
        Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
        Tuple[
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)],
            Annotated[StrictFloat, Field(gt=0)]
        ]
    ] = None,
    _request_auth: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _content_type: Optional[StrictStr] = None,
    _headers: Optional[Dict[StrictStr, Any]] = None,
    _host_index: Annotated[StrictInt, Field(ge=0, le=0)] = 0,
) -> ApiResponse[UnlockOrderResult]:
    """Unlocks a newly cloned order.

    When an order is cloned only the necessary information is copied.  In order to make changes to the order, it must be unlocked first.  Unlocking the order will clone the entire dataset and its datapoints.

    :param order_id: The id of the order to unlock
    :type order_id: str
    :param _request_timeout: timeout setting for this request. If one
                             number provided, it will be total request
                             timeout. It can also be a pair (tuple) of
                             (connection, read) timeouts.
    :type _request_timeout: int, tuple(int, int), optional
    :param _request_auth: set to override the auth_settings for an a single
                          request; this effectively ignores the
                          authentication in the spec for a single request.
    :type _request_auth: dict, optional
    :param _content_type: force content-type for the request.
    :type _content_type: str, Optional
    :param _headers: set to override the headers for a single
                     request; this effectively ignores the headers
                     in the spec for a single request.
    :type _headers: dict, optional
    :param _host_index: set to override the host_index for a single
                        request; this effectively ignores the host_index
                        in the spec for a single request.
    :type _host_index: int, optional
    :return: Returns the result object.
    """ # noqa: E501

    _param = self._order_unlock_post_serialize(
        order_id=order_id,
        _request_auth=_request_auth,
        _content_type=_content_type,
        _headers=_headers,
        _host_index=_host_index
    )

    _response_types_map: Dict[str, Optional[str]] = {
        '200': "UnlockOrderResult",
    }
    response_data = self.api_client.call_api(
        *_param,
        _request_timeout=_request_timeout
    )
    response_data.read()
    return self.api_client.response_deserialize(
        response_data=response_data,
        response_types_map=_response_types_map,
    )
